A set of step-by-step instructions for solving a specific problem
A collection of elements stored at contiguous memory locations
The server-side of an application that handles data logic and database operations
The client-side of an application that users interact with directly
An organized collection of structured data stored electronically
A program that translates source code into machine language
A program that executes instructions written in a programming language directly
A storage location paired with an associated symbolic name
A block of organized and reusable code used to perform a single action
A blueprint or template for creating objects in object-oriented programming
An instance of a class containing real values instead of variables
A mechanism where a new class adopts attributes and methods of an existing class
The ability of a variable, function, or object to take on multiple forms
The bundling of data and the methods that operate on that data into a single unit
Hiding complex implementation details and showing only the essential features
Application Programming Interface, a set of rules allowing apps to talk to each other
A platform or structure providing generic functionality to develop applications
A collection of non-volatile resources used by computer programs
A central location where data or source code is stored and managed
A command used to save changes made to the source code to a repository
A parallel version of a repository contained within the same directory
The act of combining two or more branches of code into one
An issue that occurs when changes from different branches clash with each other
The set of rules that defines the combinations of symbols in a language
The meaning of a piece of code or expression in a programming language
The process of finding and resolving bugs or defects in a computer program
An anomalous or exceptional condition requiring special processing
The smallest unit of execution within an operating system process
An instance of a program being executed by a computer
Operations that occur independently of the main program flow
Operations that occur sequentially and block subsequent tasks
A function passed as an argument to another function to be executed later
An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an async operation
A function that retains access to its lexical scope even when executed outside
Software that acts as a bridge between an operating system or database and apps
The process of making an application available for use on a server
The service of providing storage space and access for websites or applications
A computer or system that provides data or services to other computers
A piece of computer hardware or software that accesses a service made available by a server
A system of rules that allow two or more entities to transmit information
Hypertext Transfer Protocol, the foundation of data communication for the World Wide Web
A secure version of HTTP that uses encryption for secure communication
A unique string of characters that identifies each computer using the Internet
Domain Name System, the phonebook of the Internet that translates domain names to IP addresses
A small piece of data stored on the user's computer by the web browser
A temporary and interactive information interchange between two communicating devices
An object that encapsulates a security identity and grants access privileges
The process of verifying the identity of a user or process
The process of giving someone permission to do or have something
The process of converting information or data into a code to prevent unauthorized access
The process of taking encoded or encrypted text and converting it back into readable text
A function that converts an input of letters and numbers into a fixed-length encrypted output
Adding random data to the input of a hash function to safeguard passwords
Structured Query Language, used to manage and manipulate relational databases
A non-relational database management system that does not require a fixed schema
The skeleton structure that represents the logical view of the entire database
A request for data or information from a database table or combination of tables
A database structure that speeds up data retrieval operations on a table
An attribute or set of attributes that uniquely identifies a record in a database
JavaScript Object Notation, a lightweight format for storing and transporting data
Extensible Markup Language, a markup language that defines a set of rules for encoding docs
A human-readable data-serialization language commonly used for configuration files
User Interface, the series of screens, pages, and visual elements that users interact with
User Experience, the internal experience that a person has as they interact with a product
A web design approach that makes web pages render well on a variety of devices
Document Object Model, a programming interface for web documents
Single Page Application, a web app that interacts with the user by dynamically rewriting the page
Server-Side Rendering, the ability of an application to contribute the HTML files on the server
Client-Side Rendering, rendering pages in the browser using JavaScript
A technique in which client-side JavaScript sets up a static HTML page into a dynamic one
A lightweight JavaScript representation of the Document Object Model
An object that holds some information that may change over the lifetime of the component
Inputs to a reusable component that should not be changed inside that component
A special function that lets you use state and other React features without writing a class
A predictable state container designed to help write JavaScript apps that behave consistently
A modular, reusable building block for a user interface
The process of selecting a path for traffic in a network or between web pages
Software Development Kit, a collection of software development tools in one installable package
Integrated Development Environment, a software application that provides comprehensive facilities
A distributed version control system to track changes in source code
A cloud-based service that helps developers store and manage their code using Git
Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment, a method to frequently deliver apps to customers
A platform that uses OS-level virtualization to deliver software in packages called containers
A standard unit of software that packages up code and all its dependencies
An open-source container-orchestration system for automating app deployment and scaling
A project management methodology characterized by the division of tasks into short periods
A framework within which people can address complex adaptive problems
A set period of time during which specific work has to be completed
A set of practices that combines software development and IT operations
An architectural style that structures an application as a collection of services
A traditional unified software application that is self-contained and independent
A device that distributes network or application traffic across a cluster of servers
A hardware or software component that stores data so that future requests can be served faster
Content Delivery Network, a geographically distributed network of proxy servers and data centers
A network security device that monitors incoming and outgoing network traffic
A file that records either events that occur in an operating system or software runs
A copy of computer data taken and stored elsewhere so that it may be used to restore the original
Model-View-Controller, a software architectural pattern for implementing user interfaces
Representational State Transfer, an architectural style for providing standards between computer systems
A data query and manipulation language for APIs, and a runtime for fulfilling queries
